findings.title: Missing Authorization on Password Generation Endpoint
findings.description: The Get endpoint in PasswordController is missing authorization, allowing any unauthenticated user to generate passwords.
findings.attack_scenario: An attacker can make unlimited requests to the password generation endpoint, potentially exhausting server resources or generating passwords for malicious purposes. This could lead to denial-of-service or abuse of the password generation service.
findings.remediation: Add the [Authorize] attribute to the Get method in PasswordController.cs to require authentication before allowing password generation.

findings.title: Missing Authorization on Favicon Retrieval Endpoint
findings.description: The Get endpoint in FaviconsController is missing authorization, allowing any unauthenticated user to retrieve favicons.
findings.attack_scenario: An attacker can access favicon data without authentication, potentially exposing internal resources or enabling enumeration attacks. This could reveal information about the application's structure or internal systems.
findings.remediation: Add the [Authorize] attribute to the Get method in FaviconsController.cs to require authentication before allowing favicon retrieval.

findings.title: Missing Authorization on Session Refresh Endpoint
findings.description: The RefeshSession endpoint is missing authorization, allowing any unauthenticated user to refresh their session.
findings.attack_scenario: An attacker can make unauthorized session refresh requests, potentially extending session lifetimes or bypassing authentication mechanisms. This could allow unauthorized access to protected resources through session manipulation.
findings.remediation: Add the [Authorize] attribute to the RefeshSession method in SessionController.cs to require authentication before allowing session refresh.
